title: phaeneilema triquetrum
intro: Phaeneilema triquetrum, also known as the triangular grass or grass of the three corners, is a type of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. Phaeneilema triquetrum is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential side effects.
